skill.name: youtube-transcript-extractor-api-skill
skill.description: Extracts YouTube video transcripts and metadata through the BrowserAct API using a local Python helper script.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube transcripts and video metadata with BrowserAct.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly found network access, environment access, and documented script execution. Review found no evidence of prompt injection, obfuscation, hardcoded secrets, weak cryptography, or confirmed malicious intent. The main risk is expected but material: the skill sends a user-provided YouTube URL and BrowserAct API key to an external BrowserAct service.

medium_findings.title: BrowserAct API Key Sent to External Service
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment, builds a Bearer authorization header, and sends requests to the BrowserAct workflow API. This is expected for the skill, but it exposes a sensitive token to a third-party service and should be clearly disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly constructs an Authorization header from the environment variable and uses it in outbound requests. This confirms external credential use, although the endpoint matches the declared BrowserAct purpose.

medium_findings.title: User-Provided URL Forwarded to BrowserAct Workflow
medium_findings.description: The script accepts a command-line target URL and places it into the BrowserAct workflow input payload. This supports the advertised functionality, but users should understand that the video URL is submitted to BrowserAct for processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data flow from sys.argv to the workflow input payload is explicit. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it is still a clear third-party data transfer.

medium_findings.title: API Key May Be Requested Through Conversation
medium_findings.description: The instructions and error message tell the user to provide the BrowserAct API key or set it as an environment variable. Requesting a secret through an agent conversation increases accidental disclosure risk; environment configuration is safer.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording explicitly asks for the API key if the environment variable is missing. This is a concrete secret-handling concern, even without evidence of malicious exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Script Execution
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run a local Python script with a target URL argument. Static analysis flagged external command patterns, but review found a fixed script command and no shell construction inside the Python file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is plainly documented and limited to the bundled script. Some operational risk remains if an agent passes an untrusted URL without safe quoting.

low_findings.title: Static High-Severity Heuristics Mostly Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, generic secret names, reconnaissance, and a dangerous combination heuristic. Review found no cryptographic operations, no hardcoded secret value, and no prompt injection attempt; the combination is explained by normal API workflow behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files show ordinary documentation and API client code. Confidence is high enough to dismiss malicious intent, but the network plus credential pattern still warrants a medium overall rating.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-Bearing Outbound HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script sends authenticated HTTP requests to the BrowserAct API using a Bearer token from BROWSERACT_API_KEY.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Authorization header and outbound requests are directly visible. The pattern is required for the integration, but it is a sensitive behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command-Line Input Sent to Third-Party API
dangerous_patterns.description: The target URL is taken from the command line and forwarded as a BrowserAct workflow input parameter.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The source and sink are both explicit in the script. No sanitization is shown, though the value is passed in JSON rather than a shell command.

content.value_statement: YouTube transcript collection can be slow when each video must be opened and copied manually. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow that returns transcript text and key video metadata from a YouTube URL.
actual_capabilities: Runs a bundled Python script for a YouTube video URL.
Starts a BrowserAct workflow template for transcript extraction.
Polls BrowserAct task status until completion or timeout.
Returns transcript text when the API response includes it.
Can return video title, publisher, channel link, video URL, and like count when available.
Reports missing or invalid BrowserAct API key errors.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends the target YouTube URL and API key to BrowserAct.
Depends on BrowserAct API availability and workflow behavior.
May fail for videos without accessible transcripts or restricted content.

use_cases.title: Summarize Long Videos
use_cases.description: Collect a transcript before asking an AI assistant to summarize the main points.

use_cases.title: Build Content Datasets
use_cases.description: Gather transcript text and metadata for selected YouTube videos.

use_cases.title: Prepare Editorial Notes
use_cases.description: Extract channel, title, and transcript details for review and citation planning.

prompt_templates.title: Extract One Transcript
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript and available metadata from this YouTube video: [URL].

prompt_templates.title: Summarize After Extraction
prompt_templates.scenario: Use transcript output for a short summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript from [URL], then summarize the main points in five bullets.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Multiple Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract transcripts and metadata for these YouTube URLs, then compare their topics and publishers: [URLs].

prompt_templates.title: Create Research Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript and metadata from [URL], identify named entities, list key claims, and note any missing transcript sections.

output_examples.input: A YouTube URL for an interview video.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns the video title, publisher, channel link, and a readable transcript for analysis.

output_examples.input: A YouTube URL for a lecture.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns transcript text that can be summarized, searched, or converted into study notes.

output_examples.input: A video where BrowserAct cannot access the transcript.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the API error or missing transcript condition instead of inventing content.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable before using the skill.
Use direct YouTube video URLs rather than playlist or channel URLs.
Review transcripts for accuracy before publishing summaries or citations.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into shared chat logs or documents.
Do not use the skill for private or restricted videos without permission.
Do not assume transcript output is complete for every video.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to call the BrowserAct workflow API.

faq.question: Does it contact an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends requests to BrowserAct to run and retrieve the transcript workflow.

faq.question: What data can it return?
faq.answer: It can return transcript text and metadata such as title, publisher, channel link, URL, and like count.

faq.question: Can it process any YouTube video?
faq.answer: No. It depends on transcript availability, access restrictions, and BrowserAct workflow support.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses network access and an API key.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
